commands.review_summary:
The branch changes `buildStatusText` so explicit session model overrides choose the selected provider for usage summaries while preserving active-runtime usage for auto fallback/runtime-alias cases, plus a regression test.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +75. Total +86 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and issue evidence. Current main derives the usage provider from the stale active runtime when selected and active models differ, and the linked report shows the resulting OpenAI model plus DeepSeek balance mismatch in v2026.6.6.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Not a repair-lane candidate: the branch is focused and has no actionable code finding, but the external contributor still needs real behavior proof before merge.

target.title: fix(status): use selected model for usage
target.body:
## Summary

- Make `/status` usage probes follow a user-selected session model instead of a stale active runtime snapshot.
- Keep active-runtime usage behavior for auto fallback/runtime-alias cases.
- Add regression coverage for an OpenAI session override with stale DeepSeek runtime state.

Fixes #93322.

## Root Cause

`buildStatusText` chose the usage provider from the active runtime provider whenever the active runtime model differed from the selected model. After `/model`, the stored runtime snapshot can still describe the previous/default model until a later turn refreshes it, so `/status` could show the selected OpenAI model while querying and displaying DeepSeek usage.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: `/status` usage now follows the session-selected model after a user `/model` switch instead of showing usage from a stale runtime provider.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm format:check -- src/status/status-text.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`; `git diff --check -- src/status/status-text.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The new regression constructs a user-selected `openai/gpt-5.5` status request with stale `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` runtime fields and a mocked DeepSeek balance response.

Observed result after fix: The regression asserts `Model: openai/gpt-5.5`, OpenAI usage (`Usage: 5h 91% left`), no `Balance ¥22.75`, and that usage was queried for `openai` rather than `deepseek`.

What was not tested: No live Telegram run. GitHub CI has not run yet. Local autoreview was attempted but failed before reviewing because the local Codex CLI rejected config `service_tier = default`.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm format:check -- src/status/status-text.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`
- `git diff --check -- src/status/status-text.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`
- Attempted `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`; blocked before review by local Codex CLI config error: `unknown variant default, expected fast or flex in service_tier`.
